mechanism_narrative: A high level of the unemployment rate indicates slack in the labor market, which depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between UNRATE level and forward 20-day returns is positive over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is above its median.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the federal funds rate above the near-zero floor signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle, which increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 0.25% and rising for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: In more than 50% of trigger instances (FEDFUNDS rising above 0.25% for 3 months), the forward 60-day S&P 500 return is positive, or the average return is not significantly different from zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines while retail sales rise, the resulting demand-supply mismatch forces businesses to draw down inventories or cut production, signalling near-term economic weakness and predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change negative and RSAFS month-over-month change positive
failure_condition: In more than 40% of trigger instances, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive, or the pseudo-Sharpe ratio of the strategy is negative over a 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: In a low federal funds rate regime (FEDFUNDS below 1%), rising housing starts confirm strong residential investment and consumer confidence, boosting economic growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS < 1%, the forward 60-day return following positive month-over-month HOUST changes is negative in more than 50% of cases, or the rank correlation is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of the Consumer Price Index signals elevated inflation, which erodes purchasing power and forces the central bank to eventually tighten policy, reducing real earnings expectations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between CPIAUCSL level and forward 20-day returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window, or the decile spread in returns is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A robust monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (above 150,000) signals a strengthening labor market, rising incomes, and solid consumer demand, which supports corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In 50% or more of months with payroll growth > 150,000, the forward 20-day S&P 500 return is negative, or the average return is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is positive but retail sales growth turns negative, it suggests that the consumption component of the economy is faltering while production is still high, leading to excess inventories and a subsequent economic slowdown that depresses S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth positive and RSAFS month-over-month growth negative
failure_condition: In more than 40% of trigger instances, the forward 60-day S&P 500 return is positive, or the strategy's cumulative return over a 5-year walk-forward is negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 7%), further increases in the unemployment rate deepen consumer distress and deflationary pressures, leading to sharply negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants react negatively to worsening conditions.
failure_condition: In weeks where UNRATE is above 7%, the forward 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative at the 5% level.
